Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. LLM Architecture Gallery 300 upvotes, 22 comments
  2. A Visual Introduction to Machine Learning (2015) 331 upvotes, 29 comments
  3. The Linux Programming Interface as a university course text 54 upvotes, 3 comments
  4. Bandit: A 32bit baremetal computer that runs Color Forth [video] 35 upvotes, 2 comments
  5. A Plain Anabaptist Story: The Hutterites 31 upvotes, 3 comments
  6. Learning athletic humanoid tennis skills from imperfect human motion data 133 upvotes, 27 comments
  7. Electric motor scaling laws and inertia in robot actuators 12 upvotes, 0 comments
  8. Canada's bill C-22 mandates mass metadata surveillance of Canadians 487 upvotes, 129 comments
  9. How I write software with LLMs 42 upvotes, 6 comments
  10. Nasdaq's Shame 227 upvotes, 76 comments
  11. SpiceCrypt: A Python library for decrypting LTspice encrypted model files 12 upvotes, 1 comments
  12. Chrome DevTools MCP (2025) 401 upvotes, 174 comments
  13. What to know about floating-point arithmetic (1991) [pdf] 15 upvotes, 2 comments
  14. Bus travel from Lima to Rio de Janeiro 137 upvotes, 54 comments
  15. //go:fix inline and the source-level inliner 124 upvotes, 49 comments
  16. The 49MB web page 380 upvotes, 193 comments
  17. Separating the Wayland compositor and window manager 247 upvotes, 120 comments
  18. Stop Sloppypasta 177 upvotes, 95 comments
  19. Glassworm is back: A new wave of invisible Unicode attacks hits repositories 237 upvotes, 149 comments
  20. What is agentic engineering? 88 upvotes, 51 comments
  21. What makes Intel Optane stand out (2023) 191 upvotes, 128 comments
  22. Type systems are leaky abstractions: the case of Map.take!/2 38 upvotes, 19 comments
  23. Cannabinoids remove plaque-forming Alzheimer's proteins from brain cells (2016) 81 upvotes, 50 comments
  24. The emergence of print-on-demand Amazon paperback books 114 upvotes, 78 comments
  25. Federal Right to Privacy Act – Draft legislation 40 upvotes, 23 comments
  26. LLMs can be exhausting 128 upvotes, 94 comments
  27. An experiment to use GitHub Actions as a control plane for a PaaS 12 upvotes, 4 comments
  28. A new Bigfoot documentary helps explain our conspiracy-minded era 56 upvotes, 40 comments
  29. AI tools are making me lose interest in CS fundamentals 47 upvotes, 38 comments
  30. Quillx is an open standard for disclosing AI involvement in software projects 10 upvotes, 8 comments

Last generated 12:30pm, 16 March 2026. Source here.
